reasoning: The most recent data point (3.76 on Oct 24) is a significant local low compared to earlier October values (3.78-3.82), pushing the FRED statistical trend model to estimate a 68% probability of remaining above 3.76 by the first resolution date. While the FOMC rate cut on Oct. 29 is expected, it is heavily priced in, so near-term mean reversion is highly likely. As we look toward November 25, the market will increasingly price in the anticipated December cut (where FED futures show ~90% chance of a cut to 3.50-3.75%), exerting downward drift on the 6-month yield and pulling the probability lower. By January 24, 2026, the December cuts will likely have materialized, anchoring the 6-month yield lower (closer to 3.50-3.65), meaning the probability of staying above the 3.76 threshold flips below 50%. The probabilities have been chosen such that uncertainty mechanically increases (distance to 0.50 decreases) as the horizon expands, as instructed.
